issues.title: v0.5.0 Technical audit: coverage gaps and P0/P1/P2 items
issues.description:
Technical audit of v0.4.5 identified significant coverage gaps and unimplemented features to address before v0.5.0.

## Coverage statistics

| Category | Files | Tests | Coverage |
|----------|-------|-------|----------|
| CLI Commands | 84 | 3 | 3.6% |
| Adapters | 61 | 3 | 4.9% |
| Exporters | 20 | 3 | 15% |
| Services | 153 | sparse | <30% |
| **Total** | **631** | **114** | **~18%** |

## Critical gaps (P0) - DONE

### 1. CLEAR framework evaluation
- Full implementation: runAgent injection, goldenPairs, correctness/latency/efficiency/accuracy/robustness with real logic.

### 2. Export tests
- Un-skipped and aligned: langchain, crewai, gitlab-agent, docker, kubernetes, npm, drupal. json/backup still skipped (CLI feature gap).

## P1: Skipped test suites
- DONE: validation.service.test.ts, conformance.service.test.ts, profile-loader.service.test.ts (un-skipped; conformance profiles added under spec/v0.3/conformance/profiles).
- REMAIN: anthropic.adapter.test.ts, langchain-migration.service.test.ts, langgraph.validator.test.ts, crewai.validator.test.ts, mcp-integration.test.ts (schema alignment or refactor pending).

## P1: Code quality - DONE
- taxonomy.service.ts: confidence computed from facets matched.
- opentelemetry.adapter.ts: console metric exporter throws clear error.
- version-detection.service.ts: major migration path steps through majors.
- cursor/adapter.ts: left as-designed (generated stubs for user implementation).

## P2: Infrastructure - DONE
- File watching: agents-md.service.ts and llms-txt.service.ts use fs.watch with debounce.
- Release automation: Slack webhook (SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL) and RELEASE_EMAIL placeholder in release-buttons.ts.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Test coverage 50%+
- [ ] All P0 items resolved
- [ ] No skipped test suites (or documented and tracked)
- [ ] CLEAR framework runnable (stubs implemented)

---

## Roadmap: 100% coverage, Wizard (Drupal/ECA), Symfony AI Agent

### 1. 100% test coverage (priority)
- New unit tests added: perfect-agent-utils, common-file-generator, agents-md-generator.service, template-engine, openapi-adapter.
- Next batches: adapters (crewai, langflow, langchain), deploy drivers (k8s, docker), validators (cursor, anthropic, langgraph), services (git, migration, team-generator, test-generator).
- CLI commands: add integration or unit tests per command (export, validate, wizard, agents-md, etc.).
- Ramp thresholds in jest.config: 60% -> 70% -> 80% -> 90% -> 100% as batches land.

### 2. Wizard: Drupal Tool plugins + ECA models from OSSA
- Extend CLI wizard (or new step) to generate:
  - Drupal Tool plugins: PHP classes implementing ToolInterface, mapped from spec.tools in manifest.
  - ECA models: Event-Condition-Action config (YAML/JSON) for Drupal ECA module, driven by manifest triggers/autonomy.
- Output: add to existing drupal platform export or new --platform drupal-tools / --platform eca.
- Reference: Drupal ai_agents, ECA module APIs; OSSA spec.tools and spec.autonomy as source of truth.

### 3. Refactor: Symfony AI Agent (Chat + MessageStore) for ai_agents state
- Adopt symfony/ai-agent component: Chat and MessageStore for persistent conversation state.
- Map ai_agents (Drupal or standalone) to MessageStore-backed Chat so agent runs have durable history.
- Dependency tree: add symfony/ai-agent (or equivalent); refactor runtime adapters to use Chat/MessageStore instead of ad-hoc state.
- Separation: state persistence lives in Symfony component; OSSA defines manifest/config only.

/label ~"technical-debt" ~testing ~audit
/milestone %v0.5.0
